context: Tests whether the agent migrates test imports from direct vitest to vite-plus/test, updates test scripts correctly, avoids invented commands, and handles coverage migration in a coordinated way.

checklist.name: vite-plus/test imports
checklist.description: Updated test files import from `vite-plus/test` instead of directly from `vitest` (e.g. `import { describe, it, expect } from 'vite-plus/test'`)

checklist.name: vp test in scripts
checklist.description: The `test` script in package.json is updated to use `vp test` (not `vitest run`, not `pnpm exec vitest`)

checklist.name: No invented vp subcommands
checklist.description: The output does NOT contain invented commands such as `vp vitest`, `vp exec vitest`, or `vp run vitest`

checklist.name: Watch script uses vp test watch
checklist.description: The watch-mode test script (if present) uses `vp test watch` (the documented subcommand) and not `vp test --watch`, raw `vitest`, or any direct vitest invocation

checklist.name: Coverage script uses vp test run --coverage
checklist.description: The coverage script (if present) uses `vp test run --coverage` (the documented subcommand form for one-shot coverage runs) rather than `vp test --coverage` or a direct vitest coverage invocation

checklist.name: Coverage migrated together
checklist.description: Coverage configuration and test-command wiring are updated in the same set of changes as the test script migration (both appear in the output artifacts)

checklist.name: No direct package manager test call
checklist.description: The output does NOT recommend running tests via `pnpm test`, `npm test`, or `yarn test` in updated scripts or documentation

checklist.name: Coverage-v8 caveat documented
checklist.description: The output notes or acknowledges that mixed-version warnings from `@vitest/coverage-v8` are a known Vite+ limitation (not a repo bug), in migration notes or comments
